title: Online Sensor Calibration / Drift Adaptation
description: Keep in Survey: learning-based online calibration is a cross-cutting data-quality and deployment problem, not a one-off camera calibration task.
summary: Survey-stage proposal for online sensor calibration and drift adaptation. Temperature, magnetic field, lighting, vibration, sensor warm-up, mounting changes, and aging can shift camera geometry/photometry, IMU or magnetometer bias, force/tactile baselines, and proximity/depth measurements. The goal is to estimate a calibration state plus confidence online, correct observations only when observable, and expose the result to Real-Robot Infra, UMI/Tri-View, VTLA, and human-data XLA schemas.

details.text: Core idea: every robot episode should carry not only raw sensor streams, but also a live calibration state. For cameras this can include intrinsics, distortion, extrinsics, rolling-shutter/readout, depth scale, exposure/gain, and photometric state. For IMU/magnetometer this includes bias, scale, axis misalignment, hard/soft-iron effects, and local magnetic confidence. For tactile/force/proximity sensors this includes zero offset, temperature drift, hysteresis, crosstalk, contact baseline, and confidence.

Method framing: do not replace classical calibration with an unconstrained neural net. Use offline calibration as the anchor, then learn residual drift models and online confidence from self-supervised consistency: reprojection error, cross-view epipolar/hand-eye consistency, robot kinematics, VIO/SLAM residuals, fiducial or ChArUco checks, depth-to-RGB alignment, tactile/contact-force consistency, and metadata such as temperature, gain, illumination, magnetic disturbance, and time since warm-up.

Learning-based references are already credible for pieces of the stack. CalibNet and DST-Calib show self-supervised, target-free LiDAR-camera extrinsic calibration directions; Deep-BrownConrady and Calibration Tokens show learned camera-parameter or calibration-token adaptation; online VINS self-calibration gives the observability warning: calibration parameters are only trustworthy when motion sufficiently excites the relevant axes.

Temperature and light need separate treatment. Temperature can cause physical drift in camera/depth/force sensors; Basler's thermal drift documentation and F/T temperature-compensation work show that measurement error can be temperature-dependent. Lighting mostly changes photometric distribution, feature quality, and model behavior; it should feed exposure/photometric calibration or test-time perception adaptation rather than silently changing geometric camera intrinsics.

Magnetic and inertial drift should be treated as state estimation plus environment memory. Dynamic magnetometer calibration and MAGYC show that magnetometer/gyro bias can be estimated online or incrementally; magnetic distortion resistant orientation estimation suggests keeping a location-conditioned magnetic map or confidence field. For wearable human-data capture, this matters because gloves, IMUs, VIVE trackers, and body sensors may move through electrically noisy lab environments.

First v0 experiment: use the lab D405 / RGB-D camera path plus a stable workspace target as the smallest measurable benchmark. Record baseline offline calibration, then introduce warm-up/temperature, lighting, slight mount disturbance, and magnetic/electrical interference conditions. The online adapter should output corrected calibration, confidence, and a reject/rollback decision. Metrics: reprojection error, depth-to-RGB alignment, robot-frame pose error, VIO/SLAM residual, downstream policy/world-model sensitivity, and false correction rate.

Portfolio routing: Real-Robot Infra should implement the final monitor only after this survey defines the v0 spec. UMI/Tri-View needs calibration confidence in manifests so world-model failures are not misread as model failures. VTLA needs tactile/force drift state for contact labels. Human Intention Data Collection should log calibration confidence for every wearable modality before XLA consumes the signals.

risks_decisions: Survey decision 2026-06-09: keep as a new Survey card. This is bigger than Real-Robot Infra because it defines calibration-state learning, observability, confidence, and data-schema policy across sensors.
Do not let online learning silently rewrite calibration. Every update needs confidence, observability checks, bounded parameter deltas, rollback, and a clear re-calibration / re-capture trigger.

Separate geometry, photometry, and policy adaptation. Temperature or mounting changes can affect geometry; lighting usually affects photometry and perception quality; downstream model adaptation should not hide bad sensor calibration.

Start with camera/RGB-D plus IMU-style state before trying all sensors. Tactile, force, proximity, audio, and wearable biosignals should join only after the first benchmark shows useful confidence-gated adaptation.

A learned calibration token is useful only if it is interpretable enough for episode QA: what changed, why it changed, whether the correction is observable, and whether downstream labels remain valid.

Classical calibration remains the baseline. The learning contribution should be online residual correction, drift detection, confidence estimation, and schema integration, not claiming that a neural model can replace metrology.
asset_alt: Online sensor calibration survey diagram from environmental drift through calibration-state estimator, safety gate, and robot data consumers
asset_caption: Survey diagram: environmental drift becomes an explicit calibration state and confidence token before observations flow into robot learning.

references.title: Deep Learning for Camera Calibration and Beyond: A Survey
references.notes: Learning-based camera calibration survey covering pinhole, distortion, cross-view, cross-sensor models, benchmarks, and generalization gaps.

references.title: Online Self-Calibration for Visual-Inertial Navigation Systems: Models, Analysis and Degeneracy
references.notes: Key observability reference for camera/IMU intrinsics, spatial-temporal extrinsics, rolling shutter readout, and degenerate motions.

references.title: CalibNet: Geometrically Supervised Extrinsic Calibration using 3D Spatial Transformer Networks
references.notes: Self-supervised LiDAR-camera extrinsic calibration using geometric and photometric consistency rather than direct parameter supervision.

references.title: DST-Calib: A Dual-Path, Self-Supervised, Target-Free LiDAR-Camera Extrinsic Calibration Network
references.notes: Recent online, target-free LiDAR-camera extrinsic calibration reference with cross-modal difference-map association and generalization focus.

references.title: Deep-BrownConrady: Prediction of Camera Calibration and Distortion Parameters Using Deep Learning and Synthetic Data
references.notes: Single-image learned camera calibration and Brown-Conrady distortion parameter prediction with synthetic/real training data.

references.title: Extending Foundational Monocular Depth Estimators to Fisheye Cameras with Calibration Tokens
references.notes: Calibration-token framing: adapt foundation depth-model embeddings to calibration-induced distribution shift rather than always rectifying in image space.

references.title: Basler Thermal Drift Correction
references.notes: Industrial camera documentation showing temperature-dependent measurement drift and correction as a practical sensor-lifecycle issue.

references.title: Online Photometric Calibration of Automatic Gain Thermal Infrared Cameras
references.notes: Reference for online photometric calibration under sensor-specific image-formation shifts, useful for separating lighting/photometric adaptation from geometry.

references.title: Dynamic Magnetometer Calibration and Alignment to Inertial Sensors by Kalman Filtering
references.notes: State-estimation framing for magnetometer intrinsic/cross-sensor calibration and gyroscope bias under dynamic conditions.

references.title: Full Magnetometer and Gyroscope Bias Estimation using Angular Rates
references.notes: MAGYC reference for batch and online incremental factor-graph estimation of hard/soft-iron magnetometer bias and gyroscope bias.

references.title: Learning Online MEMS Calibration with Time-Varying and Memory-Efficient Gaussian Neural Topologies
references.notes: On-device learning reference for MEMS-IMU calibration under time-varying bias, temperature drift, aging, stress, and memory constraints.

references.title: Influence of Errors in Tactile Sensors on Some High Level Parameters Used for Manipulation with Robotic Hands
references.notes: Tactile-sensor reference for drift, hysteresis, crosstalk, nonlinearity, repeated calibration pressure, and manipulation-level parameter robustness.

references.title: Model Based In Situ Calibration with Temperature compensation of 6 axis Force Torque Sensors
references.notes: Force/torque sensor reference showing temperature drift can be significant in long experiments or environments unlike the original calibration setting.
